identification.tool_name: Cold Chain SOP & Fridge Layout Guide

tool_design.role_in_intervention: House operating standard for delivery, storage, hierarchy, FIFO, and cold-hold control.
tool_design.usage_moment: At rollout, onboarding, and whenever the cold-chain setup changes
tool_design.expected_output: Issued cold-chain SOP with site-specific hierarchy and sign-off

failure_warnings.warning: SOP is issued but not briefed or practiced.
failure_warnings.what_can_go_wrong: The standard exists on paper only.
People bypass it because it feels outdated.
early_signals: Users say it no longer fits

content:
# Cold Chain SOP & Fridge Layout Guide

**Tool ID:** T002-02  
**Block:** B002 (Cold Chain Management SOP)  
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)  
**Level:** L2  
**Format:** SOP  
**Role:** Manager / Food Safety Lead  
**Time Required:** 20 minutes to issue, 10 minutes to brief each team member  
**Version:** 2.0

---
## What This Tool Is For

Use this SOP to define how chilled and frozen stock moves safely from delivery to storage to service.

This is the house standard for:
- delivery acceptance
- 15-minute put-away discipline
- fridge hierarchy and product placement
- FIFO and date labelling
- cold-hold display rules
- escalation when the cold chain is broken

---
## Core House Rules

1. No chilled delivery is accepted without checks.
2. No accepted chilled delivery stays out of refrigeration longer than **15 minutes**.
3. Raw items never sit above ready-to-eat items.
4. Every opened or decanted high-risk item gets a date label.
5. Cold-hold display items are time-labelled and discarded at the agreed limit.

---
## Roles And Ownership

| Role | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| Receiver | Complete [T002-01] before signing any delivery |
| Shift lead | Make sure accepted stock is put away and labelled correctly |
| Manager | Maintain this SOP, review incidents, and close audit actions |
| Owner | Support supplier escalation and refrigeration capacity decisions |

---
## Delivery To Storage Flow

| Stage | Standard | Evidence Tool |
|---|---|---|
| Arrival | Check condition, dates, temperatures before signing | T002-01 |
| Acceptance | Reject or flag anything unsafe or out of spec | T002-01 / T002-03 |
| Put-away | Refrigerate within 15 minutes | T002-01 |
| Storage | Place stock by hierarchy and FIFO | T002-04 |
| Service / display | Time-label any cold-hold item | T002-05 |
| Review | Track supplier issues and audit findings | T002-03 / T002-04 |

---
## Fridge Hierarchy Guide

Use this as the default layout unless your brand or site-specific risk assessment requires tighter controls.

| Shelf / Zone | Store Here | Never Store Here |
|---|---|---|
| Top shelf | Ready-to-eat items, cakes, desserts, wrapped sandwiches | Raw ingredients, dirty containers |
| Middle shelf | Dairy, prepped fillings, labelled opened items | Unlabelled decanted tubs |
| Bottom shelf | Raw products or highest-risk spill items if used on site | Ready-to-eat foods |
| Door area | Low-risk sealed drinks or condiments only | Milk, high-risk fillings, sensitive stock |
| Floor | Nothing | Everything |

---
## Date Labelling Rules

| Situation | Label Required | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Opened original pack | Product, opened date, use-by, initials | "Mozzarella - opened 26/03 - use by 28/03 - MK" |
| Decanted into house container | Product, made / decanted date, use-by, initials | "Tuna mayo - made 26/03 - use by 27/03 - MK" |
| Cold-hold display item | Product, time out, discard time, initials | "Ham toastie - out 10:15 - discard 14:15 - MK" |

Use [T002-05] for the actual labels.

---
## Cold-Hold Display Rule

| Item Type | Max Time Out Of Refrigeration | Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Sandwiches, salads, filled rolls | Up to 4 hours | Time-label at the moment placed out |
| Cakes / desserts requiring chill | Follow manufacturer or site rule, never guess | Time-label if temporarily displayed |
| Milk at bar during service | Return quickly between rushes | Do not leave out through a whole service period |

If the time limit is reached, discard. Do not re-chill and restart the clock.

---
## Weekly Verification

Manager or shift lead checks weekly that:
- shelf labels still match reality
- FIFO is actually being used
- no out-of-date stock is present
- all opened high-risk stock is labelled
- display items are following cold-hold time rules
- repeat supplier issues are being tracked

Use [T002-04] for the formal audit.

---
## Escalation

Open [T002-03] when:
- a supplier delivers warm, damaged, or short-dated stock
- the same supplier repeats issues
- staff had to reject product at the door
- a fridge layout or labelling failure is becoming recurring rather than one-off

Use `B003` if the issue becomes a live temperature excursion or stock safety incident after acceptance.

---
## Team Briefing Sign-Off

| Team Member | Role | Briefed On | Signature / Initials | Trainer | Date |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | Delivery / Storage / Labels | | | |
| | | Delivery / Storage / Labels | | | |
| | | Delivery / Storage / Labels | | | |
| | | Delivery / Storage / Labels | | | |
| | | Delivery / Storage / Labels | | | |

---
## Connected Resources

**Core tools in this block:**
- T002-01 - Delivery Acceptance Checklist
- T002-03 - Supplier Temperature Incident Log & Escalation Record
- T002-04 - Weekly Fridge Organisation & Date Labelling Audit
- T002-05 - Cold-Hold & Date Label Pack

**Related blocks:**
- B001 - Daily Temperature Monitoring
- B003 - Temperature Excursion Response
- B004 - Cold Chain Excellence Dashboard

---

*Tool Version 2.0 - the operating standard that keeps the cold chain intact between the van and the guest.*
